name: cursor
description: Control Cursor AI code editor via CLI. Open files, folders, diffs, and manage extensions.
capabilities: Open files and folders in Cursor editor from terminal
Navigate to specific line and column positions
Compare two files using built-in diff viewer
Install, uninstall, and list Cursor extensions
Configure Cursor as your git commit editor
Set up portable mode with custom data directories
author: Dicklesworthstone
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown reference material for Cursor CLI commands. All 77 static findings are false positives. The 'backtick execution' detections are markdown code fences, not shell execution. The SSH reference is documentation only. No executable code, no network calls, no actual filesystem operations.
valueStatement: Switching between terminal and GUI to open files slows your development flow. This skill provides all Cursor CLI commands to open files, compare code, and manage extensions directly from command line.
userTitle: Control Cursor editor from your terminal
limitations: Requires Cursor editor to be installed on the system
CLI path varies by operating system
Remote development features require GUI interaction
Documentation only - does not execute commands automatically

useCases.title: Open projects without leaving terminal
useCases.description: Launch Cursor on specific files or folders directly from your shell without using mouse or GUI

useCases.title: Configure git editor integration
useCases.description: Set up Cursor as your git commit message editor for a better writing experience

useCases.title: Standardize extension installation
useCases.description: Create scripts to install required extensions across team members using CLI commands

promptTemplates.title: Open current directory
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I open the current directory in Cursor from the terminal?

promptTemplates.title: Jump to specific line
promptTemplates.prompt: Open src/app.ts at line 142 in Cursor editor

promptTemplates.title: Compare two files
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to compare old.js and new.js using Cursor diff mode

promptTemplates.title: Batch install extensions
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the command to install multiple extensions in Cursor via CLI?

bestPractices: Use the --wait flag when calling Cursor from scripts to ensure files are saved before continuing
Configure Cursor as git editor with 'git config --global core.editor cursor --wait'
Use --user-data-dir for portable setups or when managing multiple Cursor profiles
antiPatterns: Opening files without verifying the path exists first may cause silent failures
Using diff mode with only one file path results in an error
Forgetting --wait in automated scripts causes race conditions when files need saving

faq.question: Does this skill work on all operating systems?
faq.answer: Cursor is available on Windows, macOS, and Linux. The CLI path may vary by operating system.

faq.question: What if the cursor command is not found?
faq.answer: Ensure Cursor is installed and the CLI is added to your system PATH. You may need to reinstall Cursor.

faq.question: Can I use this with VS Code instead of Cursor?
faq.answer: Cursor is a VS Code fork. Use 'code' instead of 'cursor' for VS Code, as commands are similar.

faq.question: How do I install multiple extensions at once?
faq.answer: Run cursor --install-extension for each extension ID, or create a shell script to loop through a list.

faq.question: Is my code sent anywhere when using these commands?
faq.answer: This skill is documentation only. Cursor editor handles data according to its own privacy settings.

faq.question: Can I customize the keyboard shortcuts?
faq.answer: Keybindings are stored in ~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/keybindings.json on macOS.
